_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in refactoring research-quality data science code into production-grade, tested, documented, and deployable ML systems.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Skill AI-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-001 (TF, cosine: 0.520), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.510).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.483). Shared vocabulary: [data, skill]. NICE-only terms [compiling] vs WIDAI-only terms [code, deployable, documented, grade, ml, production] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.886 (P=0.921, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.067 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 15 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). "Skill in compiling data" and "Skill in refactoring research-quality data science code into production-grade, t" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.557 > STS 0.483 (gap +0.074). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [data, skill]. NICE-only: [compiling]. WIDAI-only: [code, deployable, documented, grade, ml, production].
